description: Baseline CPU / JVM memory / HTTP request rate dashboard for the task-management microservices. Hand-written as a lightweight starting point scoped to this project's exact service names (eureka-server, api-gateway, task-user-service, task-service, task-submission-service). For a much richer, community-maintained Spring Boot / Micrometer dashboard, import Grafana.com dashboard ID 4701 ("JVM (Micrometer)") via Grafana's Import screen and point it at the 'Prometheus' datasource provisioned here - that ID is referenced by the observability task this file was built for.

panels.title: CPU Usage (process_cpu_usage) per service
panels.description: Fraction of available CPU used by each JVM process (0-1).

panels.title: JVM Memory Used (jvm_memory_used_bytes) per service
panels.description: Heap + non-heap memory used, summed per service.

panels.title: HTTP Request Rate (http_server_requests_seconds_count) per service
panels.description: Requests/sec, computed as a 1m rate of the request counter.

panels.title: Business metrics: tasks & submissions
panels.description: Custom counters: tasks.created/assigned/completed (task-service) and submissions.created/accepted/declined (task-submission-service). Note: tasks.created and submissions.created are queried as tasks_total/submissions_total (not tasks_created_total/submissions_created_total) - Micrometer's Prometheus naming convention collapses any counter whose sanitized name ends in "_created" because OpenMetrics reserves that exact suffix for an auto-generated counter-creation-timestamp series, so it strips the "created" segment. Verified empirically against a live scrape; the underlying Micrometer meter is still named exactly "tasks.created"/"submissions.created" (see /actuator/metrics).

panels.title: Feign circuit breaker state (per service, per downstream)
panels.description: resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state is a 0/1 indicator gauge per (name, state) pair - one series per state, and exactly one is 1 at any time. name = downstream Feign client (userService in task-service and task-submission-service, taskService in task-submission-service); job = which microservice is calling it (which circuit breaker instance). Verified against a live /actuator/prometheus scrape.

title: Task Management - Spring Boot Overview
